title: fix: align R2 cache headers + codify 30-day lifecycle + bump media size
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Cache me if you can
gist.blurb: We tweaked our caching headers to remember your generated masterpieces forever. Less network traffic, faster loading, and Nomnom gets to eat the 30-day-old files.
gist.summary: Restored immutable, 1-year cache headers for R2-backed responses and codified the 30-day storage lifecycle rules in CI.
gist.impact: Repeat requests for the same generated media or text will load instantly from browser cache without unnecessary network revalidation.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a warehouse worker stamping files to be kept forever, while the Nomnom creature acts as the 30-day lifecycle garbage collector, eating old files.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny warehouse worker hat, stamping a glowing digital file with an infinity symbol. Nearby, the round Nomnom creature happily eats a dusty old document from a box labeled '30 DAYS'. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
